title: SniperBot Crypto Deferral / No-Action Decision Schema
description: Pure, deterministic, immutable, side-effect-free, non-authorizing, non-executing classification of externally supplied crypto-specific facts. The future evaluator owns the approved 20-step first-match precedence and mappings; this schema does not encode procedural conditionals, eligibility, asset approval, exchange or wallet selection, market-data access, strategy, risk, sizing, orchestration, broker or exchange APIs, orders, routing, execution, or runtime integration.

Outcome.description: Neither outcome represents crypto eligibility, asset approval, strategy, permission, transfer, order instruction, routing, or execution authority.

ReasonCode.description: Closed 22-value crypto-specific reason vocabulary. No UNKNOWN, Stock-specific, Options-specific, alias, hidden fallback, or free-text reason is approved. EmittableReasonCode must remain exactly compatible with this vocabulary.

RequiredAction.description: Full repository vocabulary. FOUNDER_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED and RESET_REQUIRED are vocabulary-only and are not emitted by this subject.

EmittableAction.description: The three actions a Crypto decision may emit.

CryptoLaneConfirmation.description: Optional supplied Crypto-lane confirmation only. It is never inferred from a token, coin, address, chain, exchange, wallet, account, contract, market datum, price, or balance.

GenericAssetClassContext.description: Optional contextual generic asset-class evidence only. It is opaque, independent, and non-authorizing and does not invoke or orchestrate a generic evaluator. Only CRYPTO is contextually compatible for the future evaluator.

AuthorityEvidence.description: Optional contextual EchoAuth evidence only. Its presence grants no authority. EchoAuth remains the sole permission authority; this schema does not call, validate, repair, reinterpret, broaden, grant, replace, or bypass EchoAuth.

crypto_reference.description: Opaque non-empty Crypto subject reference only; no token, symbol, address, chain, exchange, wallet, account, contract, market, or selection semantics are represented.

crypto_evidence_contradictory.description: Only this supplied flag establishes Crypto-evidence contradiction.

crypto_deferral.description: External descriptive assertion that Crypto-specific review should pause; not permission, eligibility, strategy, transfer, order, or execution.

crypto_no_action.description: External descriptive no-action posture; not approval, eligibility, asset handling, position handling, transfer, order, or execution.

crypto_restricted.description: External descriptive restriction fact only; not calculated, verified, derived, modified, approved, or treated as eligibility here.

crypto_excluded.description: External descriptive exclusion fact only; not calculated, verified, derived, modified, approved, or treated as eligibility here.

crypto_lane_confirmation.description: Optional; absence remains schema-valid and is classified as missing by the future evaluator.

correlation_reference.description: Mandatory opaque caller reference; exact equality with the decision reference is evaluator-owned.

Request.description: Closed request of externally supplied descriptive facts only. Evidence absent while current, evidence absent while sufficient, and evidence stale while sufficient remain structurally valid typed requests for future fail-closed classification. The future evaluator owns the approved 20-step order: evidence contradiction; deferral/no-action conflict; governed undefined combination; lane missing, contradictory, then not confirmed; generic context contradictory, ambiguous or invalid, not Crypto, stale, then out of scope; exclusion; restriction; authority failure; evidence missing, stale, then insufficient; external deferral; explicit no-action; ordinary no-action fallback. This request intentionally contains no token or coin eligibility, asset approval, symbol, address, chain, exchange, wallet, account, credential, custody, deposit, withdrawal, node, chain-analysis, market-data, pricing, liquidity, slippage, strategy, risk, sizing, simulation, paper-trading, broker or exchange API, order, routing, execution, or runtime-wiring field.

correlation_reference.description: Must equal the request correlation reference; exact cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

Decision.description: Closed descriptive output only. The future evaluator owns exact reference preservation, the approved first-match order, mappings, undefined classifications, context and authority sub-precedence, and correlation equality. No permission, eligibility, asset approval, strategy, risk, order, routing, broker, exchange, persistence, runtime, or execution field or behavior is represented.
